summary: Crisp sound‑board capture of a high‑energy Boston Garden show highlighted by Samson & Delilah, Uncle John’s Band and a stellar Baby Blue encore.
review: The recording is a solid sound‑board source that delivers clear guitar and vocal detail, though some listeners note the era’s thin ambience and occasional bass thinness. Musically the concert shines – Jerry’s scorching lead, Brent’s vibrant piano on “Far From Me,” and an unforgettable second set featuring Samson & Delilah, Uncle John’s Band/Playin’ and a soaring Baby Blue encore. Overall the performance is remembered as one of the era’s fan‑favorites despite a few mixed impressions of the mix.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (≈75 %) are 4–5 stars and praise both the setlist and recording clarity; a minority note thin SBD sound, pulling the overall weighted score to ~4.2.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and well‑balanced with clear guitar and vocals; some reviewers cite a “thin” early‑80s SBD sound, limited ambience and occasional bass lightness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A strong first set with solid jams (Sugaree, West L.A., Jack Straw) leads into an even hotter second set; the band’s energy peaks on Samson & Delilah and the encore leaves a lasting impression.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for “smokin” strings, powerful lead tones and heartfelt delivery of Baby Blue.
band_member_comments.Phil: A few notes mention a “little Phil‑lite” bass in places, suggesting occasional thinness but no major issues.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for his lively banter (“mouthy and ornery”) and memorable vocal moments (e.g., the “tear this old building down” chant).
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland gets strong kudos for piano work on Far From Me, Uncle John’s Band/Playin’, and overall keyboard presence.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum solo praised for its power; some reviewers describe the SBD drums as sounding “toy‑like” due to recording limitations.
